pillars.title: Model Inference
pillars.summary: Scalable serving of AI/ML models in production environments

pillars.title: Model Customization
pillars.summary: Fine-tuning and adaptation of foundation models for specific use cases

pillars.title: Management, Observability & Security

entries.title: vLLM Model Serving
entries.shortSummary: High-performance LLM inference serving with PagedAttention for efficient GPU memory management.
entries.customerProblem: Organizations struggle to serve large language models efficiently at scale. Traditional serving approaches waste GPU memory and deliver inconsistent latency under concurrent request loads.
entries.solutionSummary: vLLM leverages PagedAttention for efficient GPU memory management, enabling 2-4x higher throughput than traditional serving approaches. Supports continuous batching, tensor parallelism, and multiple model formats out of the box.
entries.openSourceStory: Upstream vLLM project with contributions to scheduling and multi-GPU support. Works with any Kubernetes distribution.
entries.mermaidSource:
flowchart LR
  Client[Client Request] --> LB[Load Balancer]
  LB --> vLLM[vLLM Engine]
  vLLM --> PA[PagedAttention]
  PA --> GPU[GPU Memory]
  vLLM --> Sched[Continuous Batching]
  Sched --> GPU
entries.salesNotes: Key differentiator for customers comparing LLM inference solutions. Strong performance benchmarks available.

entries.title: InstructLab
entries.shortSummary: Community-driven model alignment and fine-tuning using synthetic data generation and taxonomy-based training.
entries.customerProblem: Fine-tuning foundation models requires expensive labeled datasets and deep ML expertise. Teams spend months preparing training data before they can customize a model for their domain.
entries.solutionSummary: InstructLab uses a taxonomy-driven approach where domain experts contribute knowledge in a structured format. Synthetic data generation creates training examples automatically, enabling model customization without ML expertise.
entries.openshiftStory: InstructLab training pipelines run as OpenShift jobs, leveraging GPU scheduling and resource quotas for multi-tenant training clusters.
entries.openSourceStory: Fully open-source project with community-contributed taxonomies. CLI-driven workflow runs on any Linux system with a GPU.
entries.salesNotes: Addresses the 'last mile' of model customization. Customers can contribute domain knowledge without ML backgrounds.

entries.title: Kagenti
entries.shortSummary: Multi-agent orchestration framework for running autonomous AI agents across distributed Kubernetes pods.
entries.customerProblem: Customers want to deploy autonomous AI agents that coordinate across multiple services and pods. Existing agent frameworks are designed for single-process execution and lack native Kubernetes integration.
entries.solutionSummary: Kagenti provides a Kubernetes-native agent orchestration framework where sub-agents run in separate pods with proper resource isolation, health checks, and observability. Supports multiple LLM backends and tool registries.
entries.openshiftStory: Runs across OpenShift workloads with native integration for service mesh, pod security, and GPU scheduling.
entries.openSourceStory: Upstream orchestration logic runs on any Kubernetes cluster. Agent protocol is vendor-neutral and extensible.
entries.mermaidSource:
flowchart TD
  Orch[Orchestrator Agent] --> A1[Research Agent]
  Orch --> A2[Code Agent]
  Orch --> A3[Review Agent]
  A1 --> Tools1[Web Search Tool]
  A2 --> Tools2[Code Exec Tool]
  A3 --> Tools3[Analysis Tool]
entries.salesNotes: Great fit for distributed-agent customer asks. Demonstrates Kubernetes-native AI orchestration.

entries.title: TrustyAI
entries.shortSummary: AI model explainability, fairness monitoring, and governance toolkit for production ML pipelines.
entries.customerProblem: Regulated industries require explainable AI decisions and ongoing bias monitoring. Teams lack tooling to detect drift, measure fairness, and generate audit-ready reports for deployed models.
entries.solutionSummary: TrustyAI provides SHAP-based explainability, statistical fairness metrics, and data drift detection as a Kubernetes-native service. Integrates with model serving platforms to monitor predictions in real-time.
entries.openshiftStory: Deploys as an OpenShift operator with built-in Prometheus metrics, Grafana dashboards, and integration with OpenShift AI model serving.
entries.salesNotes: Essential for regulated industry customers (finance, healthcare). Addresses EU AI Act compliance requirements.
